Slot: FACE
STA: +4 WIS: +4
SV FIRE: +7 SV DISEASE: +4 SV COLD: +7 SV MAGIC: +3 SV POISON: +4
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Deity: Mithaniel Marr
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Very good mask, great for anyone who can afford it. Whew,bet that price is sky high!

# Jun 28 2003 at 1:55 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Current market for diamonds is about 200pp each, and gold bars cost about 10pp from merchants. So this is 210pp at cost, figure reasonable selling price would be double materials, so I'd say this would reasonably sell for about 400pp.

Purch.....

I am interest in purch. one of these(as well as other wis. based items) on the Cazic-Thule server. Post here, send me a /tell or better yet, send a message to soluna_cancuna@hotmail.com.

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 08 2001 at 10:11 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I suppose the thing is marginally useful, if you happen to be a wis caster who doesn't need wis all that badly, and you'd rather trade some excess for saves instead of AC.
#Anonymous, Posted: May 07 2001 at 10:25 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) hmm, is there ever gonna be something better than the 8wis veil? This item sounded cool till i saw the stats, no ac? only 4wis? guess it does have decent resist tho

QuoteReply
# May 07 2001 at 11:11 PM Rating: Decent

View User Forum
Anonymous
i think it is nice that a resist mask gives and wis im not to sure how much resists the damond mask gives but at least they are resists which even i have seen the highest lvl people and every calss with resist gear on (bracelets rings etc.) so this item kicks *** way over a little 8 wis str veil because it gives the resist (it's like explaining to a child but i have gone throught an hour explaining to a person why sense heading is important so im talking more than i should)

Teita Bligrath
53 Necromancer
Innoruuk
▲ Page top
RE: ...
QuoteReply
# Apr 19 2003 at 3:41 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Exactly. While you'd want that 8 wis item in normal situations, this is a nice piece of resist gear that still gives you at least some wis. So instead of trading 8 wis to improve resists when you need to, noe you only need to trade away 4.

(Menuven's Cheapo Ranger Hints)
1. Psssssst.... buy a compass for 3 plat.... or catch a gnome and torture him until he tinkers one for you. (Found in multiple places... Lake Rathe, SK, or Rathe Mountains.... found one somewheres in there)
▲ Page top
RE: ...
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 4:07 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592249730222
2 posts
You're a ranger , you would think you'd know better because you have track just like druids...If your sense heading is up and your track is up; you can dead reckon! That is why it is imparative for druids and rangers to max out their sense heading skill...Or maybe you're just a kid and don't know how to dead reckon? Btw, anon is for wussies, have the guts to put your name up there.

Maddelf Almason
Veeshan Server

QuoteReply
# May 10 2001 at 10:29 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
That's why when you're a noob and are sitting around for your health and/or mana to regen, you should be madly clicking that sense heading button. :)
▲ Page top
RE: ...
QuoteReply
# Jul 10 2001 at 3:57 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248619805
27 posts
many of the classes in the game have this nifty little spel called True North, therefore sense heading for them is obsolete. however, for the non casting types i definitely advocate keeping skill as high as possible.
▲ Page top
RE: ...
QuoteReply
# May 13 2003 at 5:00 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Costs no mana, takes no casting time, and uses up no spell slot, to use sense heading instead. None of my castrs have even BOUGHT true north. I hit sense heading on all my characters while medding and/or fighting, until it's maxed for their level, and do that every time they ding.

The compass is AWESOME when your sense heading is maxed, so is the LoY mapping feature.
▲ Page top
RE: ...
QuoteReply
# Jan 19 2002 at 3:39 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592249201131
12 posts
and you obviously aren't a caster, or you'd realize that devoting one of those precious 8 spell slots to true north (which isn't really all that useful, imho) is silly, when you can spend a few moments every once in a while working sense heading
▲ Page top
RE: ...
QuoteReply
# Feb 02 2002 at 3:08 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591761033621
93 posts
Simple solution that everyone should use. Bind your uparrow key to the ability you have sense heading at. So keep tapping up when you move...you get sense heading maxed in no time.
▲ Page top
RE: ...
QuoteReply
# Mar 25 2002 at 10:44 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591767079068
Sage
266 posts
Another option is to add it to your hotkeys, particularly the ones you use a lot. My cleric had it on his heal hotkeys, which looked something like:

/sit off (makes you stand)
/gsay healing %t (announces to group the heal on the targetted person)
/cast 3 (the heal spell, which I always kept in spell slot 3)
/doability 1 (sense heading)
/sit (sits you back down)

Parentheses added for expanation. don't put them in your hotkey.

So every time I healed someone, it gave me my direction as well. This way I had my sense heading always maxxed, and it's now at 200, which I believe is the absolute cap, since I have not had a skillup in some time.

I also threw it at the bottom of my fishing hotkey, assist hotkey, and auction hotkeys if I had a free line.
